channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La ruta de los loros en el antiguo Perú
item.description: Un nuevo estudio ha llegado a la conclusión de que los loros vivos eran objeto de comercio a lo largo y ancho de los Andes, debido a su plumaje.
item.dc:creator: Alexa Robles-Gil

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The researchers identified four distinct parrot species originating from the Amazon: the blue-and-yellow macaw, pictured; the scarlet macaw; the red-and-green macaw; and the mealy Amazon.

item.title: Raise Taxes on the Rich? These Rich New Yorkers Are All for It.
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani says he wants to raise taxes on New Yorkers who earn more than $1 million per year. Some millionaires actually agree with him.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Craig Kaplan, Marissa Hersh and Marc Baum of the New York chapter of Patriotic Millionaires. All of them support Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s tax plan.

item.title: Picture Prompt | Spring Fashion
item.description: What are you looking forward to wearing as the weather warms?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 11, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Late Night Makes Fun of Pete Hegseth’s Fruit Baskets
item.description: The Pentagon reportedly spent lavishly on doughnuts, ice cream machines and stickers — “and despite all that, nobody came to Pete Hegseth’s birthday party,” Stephen Colbert said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: ¿Quién es el próximo primer ministro de Nepal?
item.description: El partido de Balendra Shah obtuvo una victoria aplastante en las elecciones que siguieron a la revolución de la generación Z de Nepal.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Binod Ghimire and Sajal Pradhan

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Balendra Shah campaigning in Kathmandu, Nepal, last month.

item.title: Word of the Day: inherent
item.description: This word has appeared in 664 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Live Updates: U.S. Says It Hit Iranian Mine-Laying Vessels Near Vital Oil Passage
item.description: The war in the Middle East has choked tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for a fifth of the world’s oil. There was no sign of hostilities easing on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Nicolas Economou/Reuters

item.title: Múnich no tiene mar, pero sí una ola artificial que los surfistas añoran
item.description: A pesar de estar a unos 320 kilómetros del mar, Múnich era un lugar para surfear. Los entusiastas acudían al arroyo Eisbach para surfear en una ola artificial que ha desaparecido.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Laetitia Vançon

media:content.media:credit: Laetitia Vancon for The New York Times

item.title: Leave the Kurds Out of It
item.description: The United States and Israel have considered using Iranian Kurds as a ground force.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Geranmayeh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Hawre Khalid/Getty Images

item.title: See Where U.S. Sites Have Been Damaged in War With Iran
item.description: A New York Times analysis of satellite imagery and verified videos shows damage to more than a dozen American military sites and installations from Iran’s retaliatory strikes.
item.dc:creator: Bora Erden and Leanne Abraham
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Was Wounded Early in the War, Iranian and Israeli Officials Say
item.description: Officials say Mojtaba Khamenei’s legs were hurt, but the circumstances as well as the extent of his injuries were unclear. He has remained out of view since being announced as leader three days ago.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A rally in support of Iran’s new leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Ukraine Can Now Manufacture ‘China-Free’ Drones
item.description: The country has prioritized self-sufficiency in producing a crucial battlefield weapon, though weaning itself fully off cheaper Chinese components is difficult.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Tung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ukrainian drone operators in the Donetsk region in 2024. Drones now account for most Russian casualties, a Ukrainian commander said.

item.title: El nuevo líder supremo de Irán hereda la poderosa oficina que domina al país
item.description: Su padre, el ayatolá Alí Jameneí, convirtió lo que tradicionalmente era una oficina de asuntos religiosos en un gigante tenebroso de la seguridad nacional.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mojtaba Jameneí, hijo del exlíder supremo, el ayatolá Alí Jameneí, en Teherán en 2019.

item.title: Corrections: March 11, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, March 11, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: An iPad in Kindergarten Class? Timeout, Say Worried Parents.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, March 11, 2026.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 10, 2026
item.description: Chances are you’ll figure out what John Guzzetta’s puzzle is all about.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

item.title: Pope Accepts Resignation of U.S. Bishop Charged With Embezzlement
item.description: Bishop Emanuel Shaleta pleaded not guilty to stealing at least $270,000 from the St. Peter Chaldean Catholic Cathedral in El Cajon, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:credit: Gregory Bull/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The St. Peter Chaldean Catholic Cathedral in El Cajon, Calif., outside San Diego.

item.title: Bennie Thompson Defeats Young Challenger in Mississippi Primary
item.description: The victory by Mr. Thompson, the state’s longest-serving Black Democrat, offers a counterpoint to the broader push nationwide for younger leadership in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Bennie Thompson, the longest-serving Black Democrat in Mississippi, won his primary race for the Second Congressional District on Tuesday.

item.title: Suspect in Rihanna Shooting Is Charged With Attempted Murder
item.description: A woman fired a semiautomatic weapon at Rihanna’s home while she was there with ASAP Rocky and their three children on Sunday, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens

media:content.media:credit: Charly Triballeau/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Nobody was injured in the shooting at Rihanna’s Los Angeles mansion on Sunday.

item.title: Trump Administration to Restart Global Entry Program
item.description: The program for people traveling internationally, set to come back online at 5 a.m. Wednesday, had been paused amid the shutdown of the Homeland Security Department.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz

media:content.media:credit: Jacob Langston for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Homeland Security Department had said in late February that it would pause its T.S.A. PreCheck and Global Entry programs to “refocus department personnel on the majority of travelers.”

item.title: How Trump and His Advisers Miscalculated Iran’s Response to War
item.description: In the lead-up to the U.S.-Israeli attack, President Trump downplayed the risks to the energy markets as a short-term concern that should not overshadow the mission to decapitate the Iranian regime.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Tyler Pager and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
media:content.media:description: In response to Iranian threats, commercial shipping has come to a standstill in the Gulf, and oil prices have spiked.

item.title: Bondi Is Said to Move to Military Housing Because of Threats
item.description: The attorney general relocated from a Washington apartment to a base in the area within the past month, according to people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The immediate catalyst for moving Attorney General Pam Bondi was an increase in threats following the capture and prosecution of President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela, according to a senior official.

item.title: Missiles, Drones and Airstrikes Pound Middle East Into 12th Day of War
item.description: The effects of the war are being felt through the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Fadel Itani/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An airstrike in Beirut’s southern suburbs late Tuesday night.

item.title: Alberto Carvalho, Suspended LAUSD Chief, Denies Wrongdoing
item.description: Through his lawyers, Alberto Carvalho, who was put on leave after the F.B.I. raided his home and office, said that his actions were appropriate but that he would respect the investigative process.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler and Jill Cowan

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alberto Carvalho was placed on paid leave last month by the Los Angeles school board after armed federal agents searched his home and his office at the district’s headquarters.

item.title: Widow Gives Harrowing Testimony About Assassination of Haiti’s President
item.description: Martine Moïse was the first witness called in the long-awaited Miami trial of the people accused of plotting the murder of her husband, Jovenel Moïse, in 2021.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

media:content.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Martine Moise, speaking during the funeral of her husband, Jovenel Moise, in Cap Haitien, Haiti, in 2021.

item.title: Clayton Fuller and Shawn Harris Advance to Runoff in Marjorie Taylor Greene’s Georgia District
item.description: Clayton Fuller will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in an April runoff. Mr. Fuller’s win over a flashier Republican showed the power of the president’s support.
item.dc:creator: Richard Fausset
item.category: Shawn Harris (Georgia Democrat, Ret. Brigadier General with the United States Army)

media:content.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters, Matt Hamilton, via Chattanooga Times Free Press, via Associated Press

item.title: Woman Found Innocent After 22 Years in Prison Will Not Be Deported
item.description: Carmen Mejia was cleared of murder in connection with the death of a baby in her care, who was scalded by hot water in 2003 in Austin, Texas. Federal officials said she will be allowed to stay in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk and Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Mikala Compton/Austin American-Statesman, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Carmen Mejia entering the courtroom for a hearing in Austin, Texas, on Monday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: Trump administration officials cast the president as the sole arbiter on the U.S. war effort. International aid groups were warning of a growing humanitarian crisis in Lebanon, where nearly 700,000 people had been displaced, the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: From left, Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Vice President JD Vance at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Monday.

item.title: One Member of Iranian Soccer Squad Abandons Asylum Bid in Australia
item.description: Seven members of the Iranian women’s soccer delegation had initially opted to stay in Australia. The reversal was a sign of how fraught that decision was.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim

media:content.media:credit: Hasnoor Hussain/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Members of the Iranian women’s soccer team arriving in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, after flying from Australia.

item.title: U.S. Forces Attack Iranian Mine-Laying Ships Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A video posted by the U.S. Central Command showed munitions striking nine vessels, most of which were moored at the time. Whether any mines have been laid in Gulf waters was unclear.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Amr Alfiky/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Boats docked in a fishing area in the Strait of Hormuz in Musandam, Oman, earlier this month.

item.title: Los residentes de Irán hablan sobre la guerra: ‘No quedará nada de Teherán’
item.description: Algunos residentes dijeron que los ataques de la noche del lunes al martes fueron de los peores que habían experimentado desde que comenzó la guerra.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Irán ha enfrentado continuos bombardeos de Estados Unidos e Israel.

item.title: Bumblebee Queens Can Breathe Underwater
item.description: A new study offers clues as to how the insects survive flooding as they emerge from a hibernation-like phase every winter.
item.dc:creator: Jason P. Dinh

item.title: Matt Snell Dies at 84; Carried Jets to Stunning Upset in Super Bowl III
item.description: His pounding runs for the underdog New York team against the Baltimore Colts secured a pivotal win for the American Football League.
item.dc:creator: Jason M. Bailey

media:content.media:credit: Harold Valentine/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Matt Snell of the New York Jets carrying the ball during Super Bowl III in January 1969. Snell was the star of the game, one of the greatest upsets in pro football history.

item.title: Democrats Demand Hegseth and Rubio Testify on Iran War
item.description: Senate Democratic leaders called on President Trump to dispatch the senior cabinet officials to make the case to Congress and the American public for the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro and Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the Capitol in January.

item.title: 2 Former Police Officers Conspired to Steal From Prostitute, U.S. Says
item.description: The two men pleaded not guilty on Tuesday in federal court to charges that they violated a woman’s civil rights while responding to a complaint about a brothel in a Queens building.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Two former police officers both pleaded not guilty in Federal District Court in Brooklyn.

item.title: Iranian Military Shows It Knows How to Adapt, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: Iran appears to be targeting what it views as American vulnerabilities, including air defenses meant to guard troops and assets in the region.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran on Tuesday illustrating the succession of Iran’s three supreme leaders.

item.title: The U.S. Showers Iran With Bombs
item.description: Also, Alabama spares a man on death row. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A street in Tehran, today.

item.title: Trump Tries to Sidestep Blame for Any Civilian Deaths in Iran
item.description: The president and the Pentagon have cast blame on Iran for the mounting toll. More than 1,800 people have died in the war, including many civilians.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump has repeatedly suggested a strike on a girls’ school that reportedly killed 175 people was carried out by Iran or another country.

item.title: Mamdani’s Latest Show of Support for Mahmoud Khalil: A Dinner Invitation
item.description: It appears that a relationship that began with political solidarity over the plight of Palestinians has developed into a friendly connection between the mayor and the activist.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall

media:content.media:credit: Scott Heins for The New York Times; Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mahmoud Khalil, left, and Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: War in Iran Could Lead to Food Shortages in Region, Experts Warn
item.description: Some residents of Lebanon, Gaza and Iran are reporting shortages of food, rising food prices and other disruptions to food supplies as the conflict in the Middle East continues.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A food distribution center for internally displaced people in Dahiya, the densely populated southern suburb of Beirut that is a stronghold of Hezbollah, last week.

item.title: Boots on the Ground — but Whose?
item.description: The Kurds have been let down time and again by the U.S. Here’s why they might still partner with Americans in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Sedat Suna/Getty Images

item.title: How to Master the Single-Leg Deadlift
item.description: Try this variation of the classic move for better strength and balance.
item.dc:creator: Anna Maltby

media:content.media:credit: Theodore Tae/The New York Times

item.title: How Does This End? Four Scenarios for What Comes Next With Iran.
item.description: The best hope? Weaken the regime’s ability to lash out and resist an uprising.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: ChatGPT, Other Chatbots Approved for Official Use in the Senate
item.description: New guidelines said Senate aides could use A.I. tools for official work, including research, drafting and editing documents, and preparing briefings and talking points for lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The United States Capitol on Tuesday. The chief information officer for the Senate sergeant-at-arms said in a one-page memo that aides could use some chatbot tools for official work.

item.title: U.S. Showers Iran With Bombs in Most Intense Strikes of the War, Pentagon Says
item.description: Iranians cowered under the barrage as Pete Hegseth, the U.S. defense secretary, said the U.S. aimed to wipe out Iran’s capacity to obtain nuclear weapons “forever.”
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A new billboard in Valiasr Square in Tehran on Tuesday shows the history of Iran’s three supreme leaders: Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, his successor Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and his son Mojtaba Khamenei, who has been chosen as the new supreme leader.

item.title: ¿Te ha afectado el Tratado entre México, EE. UU. y Canadá? Queremos saberlo
item.description: Si eres empresario, agricultor, camionero, agente de aduanas o un consumidor especialmente avezado, y has experimentado de primera mano los efectos de este acuerdo comercial, queremos conocer tu opinión.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson

item.title: A Family Home in Milan’s Design District
item.description: The husband and wife Mario Milana and Gabriella Campagna walk us through their 4,000-square-foot Milan apartment, which is filled with colorful furniture and prototypes that Milana designed himself.
item.dc:creator: Paolo Lobbia and Megan Lovallo

item.title: Imprisoned in Iran, and Under Bombardment
item.description: Iranian detention centers, some holding people swept up in a government crackdown on protests along with other prisoners, have been damaged in the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The remnants of a police station on Niloufar Street in Tehran after it was hit by airstrikes this month.

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
